content: In Ephesians 2:8, 'For by grace you have been saved.' What does the Greek word for 'grace' mean?
explanation: Charis (χάρις) means 'grace, favor' — God's undeserved gift.
options: Eleos — mercy
Charis — grace, favor
Agape — love
Dikaiosune — righteousness

content: In Ephesians 6:12, 'For we do not wrestle against flesh and blood.' What does the Greek word for 'wrestle' mean?
explanation: Pale (πάλη) means 'wrestling, struggle' — a personal and inner spiritual battle.
options: Polemos — war
Agon — contest
Machomai — to fight
Pale — wrestling, struggle

content: According to Ephesians 2:8-9, by what are we saved?
explanation: Ephesians 2:8-9 — For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, not a result of works.
options: By baptism
By good works
By grace, through faith — not by works
By keeping the law

content: Ephesians 6 speaks of the whole armor of God. Why must we put it on?
explanation: Ephesians 6:11 — We battle not against flesh and blood, but against spiritual forces in the heavenly places.
options: To protect family
To attack the enemy
To be strong
To stand firm against the schemes of the devil

content: Ephesians 1:3: With what kind of blessings has God blessed us?
explanation: God's blessings are spiritual and eternal, not just material.
options: Financial blessings
Health blessings
Material blessings
Every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places

content: Ephesians 2:10: We are God's workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for what?
explanation: Saved not by works, but SAVED FOR good works.
options: Good works that God prepared beforehand
To be saved
To rest
To keep the law

content: Ephesians 4:2-3 urges us to live with one another in what way?
explanation: Maintain the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
options: Competing
Reasoning
Independently
With humility, gentleness, patience, bearing one another in love

content: Ephesians 4:26: 'Be angry and do not ___; do not let the sun go down on your anger'
explanation: You may be angry, but do not let anger lead to sin. Settle it before the day ends.
options: Think
Sin
Speak
Act

content: Ephesians 4:32: How are we to treat one another?
explanation: Forgive others because Christ has forgiven us. Forgiveness is grace lived out.
options: Cold and aloof
Justly
Kind, tenderhearted, forgiving as God in Christ has forgiven you
Harshly

content: Ephesians 5:25: How should husbands love their wives?
explanation: Marital love modeled on Christ's sacrifice for the Church. Love means giving, not getting.
options: As Christ loved the church and gave Himself up for her
As they love their parents
As they love their children
As they love themselves

content: Ephesians 6:12: We do not wrestle against flesh and blood, but against whom?
explanation: Our true battle is spiritual — against forces of darkness, not human beings.
options: Governments
Rulers, authorities, cosmic powers of darkness
Friends
People

content: What does the whole armor of God consist of?
explanation: Six pieces of spiritual armor — defensive and offensive. The Word of God is the only offensive weapon.
options: Just a shield
Belt of truth, breastplate of righteousness, shoes of the gospel, shield of faith, helmet of salvation, sword of the Spirit
Just a helmet
Just a sword

content: How does Ephesians 6:18 instruct us to pray?
explanation: Prayer is a spiritual weapon to be used continually, not only in times of need.
options: Only at church
At all times in the Spirit, with all prayer and supplication
Only in the morning
Only when in trouble

content: Ephesians 5:18: Do not be drunk with wine, but be filled with what?
explanation: Be filled with the Spirit instead of with wine. The Spirit brings joy, singing, and thanksgiving.
options: Joy
The Holy Spirit
Knowledge
The Word

content: According to Ephesians 1:13-14, with what are believers sealed as a guarantee?
explanation: Ephesians 1:13-14 — 'You... were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit, who is the guarantee of our inheritance.'
options: By baptism
By circumcision
By the promised Holy Spirit
By good works

content: By what does Ephesians 2:8-9 say we are saved?
explanation: Ephesians 2:8-9 — 'For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, not a result of works.'
options: By works
By grace, through faith — not by works
By heritage
By continual repentance

content: What did Paul pray the Ephesians might know that surpasses knowledge?
explanation: Ephesians 3:18-19 — 'May have strength to comprehend... what is the breadth and length and height and depth, and to know the love of Christ that surpasses knowledge.'
options: Worldly wisdom
The secrets of the universe
The origin of angels
The love of Christ

content: What ministry gifts does Ephesians 4:11 list as Christ's gifts to the church?
explanation: Ephesians 4:11 — 'And he gave the apostles, the prophets, the evangelists, the shepherds and teachers.'
options: Apostles, prophets, evangelists, shepherds, and teachers
Kings, priests, prophets
Judges, kings, shepherds
Elders, deacons, pastors

content: How does Ephesians 5:25 say husbands should love their wives?
explanation: Ephesians 5:25 — 'Husbands, love your wives, as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her.'
options: As their own father
As blood brothers
As Christ loved the church and gave Himself for her
As close friends

content: According to Ephesians 6:12, against whom do believers wrestle?
explanation: Ephesians 6:12 — 'For we do not wrestle against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the cosmic powers over this present darkness, against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly places.'
options: Against flesh and blood (humans)
Against pagan kings
Against heretics
Against rulers, authorities, cosmic powers of darkness, spiritual forces of evil

content: According to Ephesians 1:7, through what are we redeemed?
explanation: Ephesians 1:7 — 'In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace.' 'Redemption' (apolytrōsis) = buying back at a price; BLOOD = the price. 3 elements: in Christ + by blood + according to grace.
options: Through good works (per the textual narrative)
Through keeping the law (according to the text's framing)
Through his blood — the forgiveness of sins according to the riches of his grace
Through long repentance (as described in the passage)

content: According to Ephesians 2:13, the Gentile believers who once were far off have now become what?
explanation: Ephesians 2:13 — 'But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off have been brought near by the blood of Christ.' Contrast with 2:12 'separated from Christ, alienated from the commonwealth of Israel'. The BLOOD = the key to reconciliation of Gentiles to God and to the Jewish people.
options: Become Jews (per the textual narrative)
Brought near by the blood of Christ
Kept the law of Moses
Were circumcised

content: According to Ephesians 4:1, what does Paul (the prisoner of the Lord) urge believers to do?
explanation: Ephesians 4:1 — 'I therefore, a prisoner for the Lord, urge you to walk in a manner worthy of the calling to which you have been called.' This is the PIVOT verse of the book: chapters 1-3 = doctrine (who you ARE in Christ); chapters 4-6 = walk (what you must DO).
options: Offer many sacrifices
Separate from society
Memorize the law (per the textual narrative)
Walk worthy of the calling to which you have been called

content: According to Ephesians 4:5, there is one LORD, one FAITH, and one what?
explanation: Ephesians 4:5 — 'one Lord, one faith, one baptism.' Paul's seven 'ONES' (4:4-6): 1 body, 1 Spirit, 1 hope, 1 Lord, 1 faith, 1 baptism, 1 God. The unity of the Church is based on the TRIUNE GOD — not external uniformity.
options: One baptism
One temple
One nation
One promised land

content: According to Ephesians 5:1, whom must believers imitate as beloved children?
explanation: Ephesians 5:1 — 'Therefore be imitators of God, as beloved children.' The next verse 5:2 — 'and walk in love, as Christ loved us.' Imitate God THROUGH Christ. The only NT verse explicitly commanding 'imitate God'.
options: Imitate Paul
Imitate the patriarchs
Imitate God
Imitate the angels

content: According to Ephesians 5:8, what were believers before, and what are they now in the Lord?
explanation: Ephesians 5:8 — 'For at one time you were darkness, but now you are light in the Lord. Walk as children of light.' Note: NOT 'were in darkness' but 'WERE darkness' and 'ARE light' — the fundamental nature has changed through Christ. The evidence = fruit of light (5:9).
options: Once sinners, now righteous
Once darkness, now light — walk as children of light
Once dead, now alive
Once Gentiles, now Israel

content: According to Ephesians 1:4, when did God choose us in Christ, and for what purpose?
explanation: Ephesians 1:4 — 'Even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and blameless before him.' Election has a PURPOSE = to be HOLY (hagios) + BLAMELESS (amōmos). Grace is not a license for careless living.
options: Before birth — to be wealthy (per the textual narrative)
Before baptism — to be healed
Before hearing the gospel — to be forgiven
Before the foundation of the world — to be holy and blameless before him

content: According to Ephesians 1:5, what did God predestine for us through Christ?
explanation: Ephesians 1:5 — 'He predestined us for adoption to himself as sons through Jesus Christ, according to the purpose of his will.' 'Adoption' (huiothesia) = Roman legal term — a child taken in as heir. In the OT, Israel was 'firstborn son' (Ex 4:22); in the NT, every believer is a SON.
options: Material wealth (as described in the passage)
Long life (per the textual narrative)
Adoption as sons through Jesus Christ — according to the purpose of his will
Worldly peace (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Ephesians 1:10, what is the mystery of God's will in the fullness of time?
explanation: Ephesians 1:10 — 'as a plan for the fullness of time, to unite all things in him, things in heaven and things on earth.' 'Unite' (anakephalaiōsasthai) — gathering everything under ONE head = Christ. The ultimate goal of redemptive history: COMPLETE in Christ. Revelation 21-22 unveils this reality.
options: Take believers to heaven (as described in the passage)
Destroy the world (per the textual narrative)
Restore Israel's land (according to the text's framing)
UNITE ALL THINGS in Christ — things in heaven and things on earth

content: According to Ephesians 1:18, what does Paul pray would be opened in believers?
explanation: Ephesians 1:18 — 'having the eyes of your hearts enlightened, that you may know what is the hope to which he has called you, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints.' Paul prays not that believers would learn more — they already have Christ — but that they might KNOW (epignōsis) more deeply what they already POSSESS.
options: The eyes of the heart — to know the hope of calling, riches of glorious inheritance, and great power
Physical eyes — to see miracles (according to the text's framing)
Mind's eye — to interpret Scripture (as described in the passage)
Spiritual eyes — to see angels (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Ephesians 1:19-20, the power of God in believers is compared to what?
explanation: Ephesians 1:19-20 — 'and what is the immeasurable greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to the working of his great might that he worked in Christ when he raised him from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places.' RESURRECTION power is at work IN believers.
options: The power that created the universe
The power that brought Israel out of Egypt
The power that raised Christ from the dead and seated him at God's right hand
The power that brought down Jericho

content: According to Ephesians 2:1, what was the believers' previous state?
explanation: Ephesians 2:1 — 'And you were dead in the trespasses and sins.' NOT 'sick' or 'weak' — but DEAD (nekros). The dead cannot save themselves — only God makes alive (2:5). This is the foundation of free grace: NO inherent capacity to respond.
options: Alive but weak
Dead in trespasses and sins
Awake but lost
Healthy but useless

content: According to Ephesians 2:4-5, how is God described in saving us?
explanation: Ephesians 2:4-5 — 'But God, being rich in mercy, because of the great love with which he loved us, even when we were dead in our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ — by grace you have been saved.' 2 KEY phrases: RICH in mercy + GREAT love.
options: Stern — punishing us (per the textual narrative)
Judging — condemning us (according to the text's framing)
RICH IN MERCY — because of his great love for us, he made us alive with Christ
Patient — waiting for us (as described in the passage)

content: According to Ephesians 2:6, what has God done for believers with Christ?
explanation: Ephesians 2:6 — 'and raised us up with him and seated us with him in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus.' 'With' (syn-) = WITH Christ. Believers are ALREADY seated with Christ — a PRESENT spiritual position, not future. 3 'withs': made alive with, raised with, seated with.
options: Seated us in Jerusalem (according to the text's framing)
Seated us beside Paul (per the textual narrative)
Brought us to heaven after death
Raised us up AND seated us with him in the heavenly places in Christ

content: According to Ephesians 2:14, what is Christ for Jew and Gentile?
explanation: Ephesians 2:14 — 'For he himself is our peace, who has made us both one and has broken down in his flesh the dividing wall of hostility.' The 'wall' (mesotoichon) — alludes to the partition separating the 'Court of Israel' from the 'Court of Gentiles' in Herod's temple; crossing meant death. Christ DEMOLISHED the wall = direct access opened.
options: Our PEACE — he made the two one, breaking down the dividing wall
Conquering King — he rules over both
Atoning Sacrifice — he redeems both
A Prophet — he teaches both

content: According to Ephesians 2:19, the Gentile believers are no longer strangers and aliens, but what?
explanation: Ephesians 2:19 — 'So then you are no longer strangers and aliens, but you are fellow citizens with the saints and members of the household of God.' Two key words: FELLOW CITIZENS (sympolitēs) + MEMBERS of the household (oikeios). NOT guests at a feast — but FAMILY members of God's house.
options: Converts to Judaism (per the textual narrative)
FELLOW CITIZENS with the saints and MEMBERS of the household of God
Temporarily heavenly citizens
Partakers only after death (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Ephesians 3:6, what mystery, hidden in past ages, is now revealed about the Gentiles?
explanation: Ephesians 3:6 — 'This mystery is that the Gentiles are fellow heirs, members of the same body, and partakers of the promise in Christ Jesus through the gospel.' 3 'FELLOWS' (syn-): fellow heirs + fellow body + fellow partakers. NO second class.
options: They must become Jews to be saved (according to the text's framing)
They must keep the Mosaic law (per the textual narrative)
They are FELLOW HEIRS, FELLOW MEMBERS of the body, and FELLOW PARTAKERS of the promise in Christ
They are only permitted partial participation

content: According to Ephesians 3:20-21, what can God do compared to what we ask or think?
explanation: Ephesians 3:20-21 — 'Now to him who is able to do far more abundantly than all that we ask or think, according to the power at work within us, to him be glory in the church and in Christ Jesus.' 'Far more abundantly' (hyperekperissou) = SUPER ABUNDANT BEYOND. Prayer is never 'too much' with God.
options: A little less (per the textual narrative)
Exactly what we ask (as described in the passage)
What we imagine (according to the text's framing)
FAR MORE ABUNDANTLY beyond all — according to the power at work within us

content: According to Ephesians 4:7-8, grace has been given to each of us according to what? Given by whom?
explanation: Ephesians 4:7-8 — 'But grace was given to each one of us according to the measure of Christ's gift. Therefore it says, "When he ascended on high he led a host of captives, and he gave gifts to men."' Quoting Psalm 68:18 — Christ is the conquering King + gives the SPOILS (spiritual gifts) to his people.
options: According to our merit — given by God (per the textual narrative)
According to the measure of Christ's gift — when he ascended on high he led captives and gave gifts to men
According to piety — given by the Holy Spirit
According to rank — given by the Church (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Ephesians 4:13, what is the goal of the spiritual gifts?
explanation: Ephesians 4:13 — 'until we all attain to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to mature manhood, to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ.' Goal: MATURITY (teleios) + UNITY of faith. Unlike 'apparent spirituality' — must DEEPLY know.
options: To attain unity of faith and knowledge of the Son of God, to mature manhood, to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ
To have many miracles (according to the text's framing)
To plant many churches (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
To increase numbers (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to Ephesians 4:15, why must believers speak the TRUTH in love?
explanation: Ephesians 4:15 — 'Rather, speaking the truth in love, we are to grow up in every way into him who is the head, into Christ.' 'TRUTH in LOVE' (alētheuontes en agapē) = balanced formula. Truth alone → rigid; love alone → blind.
options: To win arguments (per the textual narrative)
To separate from the wrong
To GROW UP in all things into him who is the head, Christ
To protect the organization

content: According to Ephesians 4:22-24, what must believers PUT OFF and PUT ON?
explanation: Ephesians 4:22-24 — 'to put off your old self, which belongs to your former manner of life and is corrupt through deceitful desires, and to be renewed in the spirit of your minds, and to put on the new self, created after the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness.' Image of CHANGING CLOTHES (apothesthai/endysasthai). Renewal (anakainosis) starts in the MIND.
options: Off the old garment, on the new (as described in the passage)
Off poverty, on wealth (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Off tradition, on innovation (according to the text's framing)
Off the OLD SELF (corrupt according to deceitful desires) — put on the NEW SELF (created after the likeness of God in true righteousness + holiness)
tags: Off old self, on new self

content: According to Ephesians 4:30, what must believers avoid doing to the Holy Spirit?
explanation: Ephesians 4:30 — 'And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.' 'Grieve' (lypeō) = cause sorrow. Shows the Holy Spirit is NOT an impersonal force — but a PERSON with emotions. 'Sealed' (sphragizō) = stamp of ownership — cannot be lost.
options: Avoid boasting in him (as described in the passage)
Avoid GRIEVING the Holy Spirit — by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption
Avoid asking him (per the textual narrative)
Avoid not listening (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Ephesians 5:15-16, why must believers walk carefully and redeem the time?
explanation: Ephesians 5:15-16 — 'Look carefully then how you walk, not as unwise but as wise, making the best use of the time, because the days are evil.' 'Making the best use of the time' (exagorazomenoi ton kairon) = BUYING UP the time — wise investment. 'EVIL days' = the corrupt age full of temptation.
options: Because enemies are near
Because rewards are running out
Because the days are EVIL
Because angels are watching

content: According to Ephesians 5:21, why must believers submit to ONE ANOTHER?
explanation: Ephesians 5:21 — 'submitting to one another out of reverence for Christ.' The TRANSITION verse to 5:22-6:9 (wives/husbands, parents/children, masters/servants). Submission does NOT mean inferiority — Christ himself voluntarily submitted to the Father. Every relationship in the Church has a SERVANT element.
options: Because Paul teaches it
Because the law demands it
Because of church tradition
Out of reverence for Christ

content: According to Ephesians 6:4, what must fathers NOT do to children, and what must they do?
explanation: Ephesians 6:4 — 'Fathers, do not provoke your children to anger, but bring them up in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.' 'Provoke' (parorgizō) = stir to rebellion. Bring up = paideia (whole-life education) + nouthesia (admonition). Fathers have RESPONSIBILITY for spiritual education — not just mothers.
options: Do not provoke your children to anger — bring them up in the discipline and instruction of the Lord
Do not punish — teach Mosaic law (according to the text's framing)
Do not forbid — let them be free (as described in the passage)
Do not reward — only discipline (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Ephesians 1:14, what is the Holy Spirit for believers until the redemption of the inheritance?
explanation: Ephesians 1:14 — 'who is the guarantee of our inheritance until we acquire possession of it, to the praise of his glory.' 'GUARANTEE' (arrabōn) = down payment. The implication: if God has paid down — he WILL CERTAINLY pay in full. Synonymous with 'sealed' in 1:13.
options: A great sign (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
A random gift (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
The DOWN PAYMENT (guarantee) — securing the inheritance until the redemption of God's possession
A temporary strength (as described in the passage)

content: According to Ephesians 1:22-23, God placed all under Christ's feet and gave him as Head over what? What is the Church to Christ?
explanation: Ephesians 1:22-23 — 'And he put all things under his feet and gave him as head over all things to the church, which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.' 'PLĒRŌMA' (fullness) — the Church is where Christ EXPRESSES his fullness; Christ and Church are ONE spiritual unit.
options: Head over Israel — the Church is Christ's servant
Head over angels — the Church is Christ's branch
Head over prophets — the Church is Christ's disciple
Head OVER ALL THINGS to the Church — which is his BODY, the fullness of him who fills all in all

content: According to Ephesians 2:2, whom and what rule did believers formerly follow?
explanation: Ephesians 2:2 — 'in which you once walked, following the course of this world, following the prince of the power of the air, the spirit that is now at work in the sons of disobedience.' 'Prince of the power of the air' (archon tēs exousias tou aeros) = Satan; the air domain (between earth and heaven) = where demonic forces operate. 'Sons of disobedience' = the unsaved.
options: Following the course of this world, obeying the PRINCE OF THE POWER OF THE AIR (Satan), the spirit now at work in the sons of disobedience
Only natural inclination (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
Worldly kings (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
False prophets (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)

content: According to Ephesians 2:15-16, what did Christ abolish to create what new thing?
explanation: Ephesians 2:15-16 — 'by abolishing the law of commandments expressed in ordinances, that he might create in himself one new man in place of the two, so making peace, and might reconcile us both to God in one body.' 'ONE NEW MAN' (kainos anthrōpos) = NEW humanity — NOT Jews + Gentiles side by side, but a totally NEW unified body.
options: Abolished the old Gospel — created a new Gospel (according to the text's framing)
Abolished the law of commandments and ordinances, to create in himself ONE NEW MAN out of two (Jew + Gentile) — reconciling both to God in one body
Abolished the OT temple — created a new synagogue (as described in the passage)
Abolished Abraham's covenant — created Sinai's (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Ephesians 2:20, on what foundation is the Church built, and who is the CORNERSTONE?
explanation: Ephesians 2:20 — 'built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus himself being the cornerstone.' The cornerstone (akrogōniaios) = ORIENTS every other stone. Apostles + prophets = foundational revelation (NT + OT); Christ = the CENTER. Quoting Isaiah 28:16.
options: On Paul's foundation — Peter is cornerstone
On Moses' foundation — David is cornerstone
On the foundation of the apostles and prophets — Christ is the CORNERSTONE
On the 12 tribes — Aaron is cornerstone

content: According to Ephesians 3:8, what does Paul call himself, and what task is he given?
explanation: Ephesians 3:8 — 'To me, though I am the very least of all the saints, this grace was given, to preach to the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ.' 'VERY LEAST' (elachistoteros — superlative of 'least') = a coined word, stronger than 'least'. Paul is most humble when entrusted with the greatest task. 'UNSEARCHABLE' (anexichniastos) = cannot be traced.
options: Greatest among apostles — teach high doctrine (as described in the passage)
Pure Jew — defend Mosaic law (per the textual narrative)
Highly educated — teach philosophy (according to the text's framing)
The very LEAST of all the saints — given to preach the UNSEARCHABLE RICHES of Christ to the Gentiles

content: According to Ephesians 3:10, to whom does God reveal his MANIFOLD WISDOM through the Church?
explanation: Ephesians 3:10 — 'so that through the church the manifold wisdom of God might now be made known to the rulers and authorities in the heavenly places.' 'MANIFOLD' (polypoikilos) = many-colored. The Church is God's 'stage' DISPLAYING his wisdom to angels — including evil ones (1 Pet 1:12 angels long to look).
options: To the world (according to the text's framing)
To the rulers and authorities in the heavenly places (spiritual principalities and powers)
To the Jews (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
To later prophets (as described in the passage)

content: According to Ephesians 3:18-19, Paul prays for believers to grasp what 4 dimensions of Christ's love?
explanation: Ephesians 3:18-19 — 'may have strength to comprehend with all the saints what is the breadth and length and height and depth, and to know the love of Christ that surpasses knowledge, that you may be filled with all the fullness of God.' 4 DIMENSIONS = TOTALITY (implying infinity). Love transcends KNOWLEDGE — only spiritually grasped.
options: BREADTH, LENGTH, HEIGHT, DEPTH — to know Christ's love that surpasses knowledge, that you may be filled with all the fullness of God
East, West, North, South — the whole universe (according to the text's framing)
Beginning, present, future, eternal — all time (as described in the passage)
Earth, sea, sky, fire — all elements (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to Ephesians 4:9-10, what did Christ do before ascending on high?
explanation: Ephesians 4:9-10 — 'In saying, "He ascended," what does it mean but that he had also descended into the lower regions, the earth? He who descended is the one who also ascended far above all the heavens, that he might fill all things.' Ascending requires HAVING DESCENDED. 'Lower regions' interpretations: (a) earth; (b) Hades. Paul EMPHASIZES: Christ went to the LOWEST so as to surpass ALL.
options: He only descended to Jerusalem (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
He only descended to the temple (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
He DESCENDED into the lower regions, the earth — the one who descended is the one who also ascended above all the heavens, that he might fill all things
He only was born at Bethlehem (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to Ephesians 5:14, possibly an early hymn — what is quoted?
explanation: Ephesians 5:14 — 'Therefore it says, "Awake, O sleeper, and arise from the dead, and Christ will shine on you."' Likely quoted from an early BAPTISMAL HYMN — calling new believers out of the 'night' of sin (5:8). Similar to Colossians 3:1 (raised with Christ → seek things above).
options: 'Repent, for the kingdom is near' (per the textual narrative)
'Awake, O sleeper, and arise from the dead, and Christ will shine on you!'
'Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved'
'Love the Lord with all your heart and soul'

content: According to Ephesians 5:31-32, marriage (one flesh) is a MYSTERY about what?
explanation: Ephesians 5:31-32 — '"Therefore a man shall leave his father and mother and hold fast to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh." This mystery is profound, and I am saying that it refers to Christ and the church.' Quoting Gen 2:24. Paul reveals: God designed marriage from the BEGINNING as a SHADOW of Christ-Church relationship — not the other way. That's why marriage is sacred.
options: Mystery of creation (per the textual narrative)
Mystery of Israel + God (as described in the passage)
Mystery of Adam + Eve (according to the text's framing)
A great MYSTERY — about Christ and the Church

content: According to Ephesians 6:11, why must believers put on the whole armor of God?
explanation: Ephesians 6:11 — 'Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil.' 'SCHEMES' (methodeia) = sophisticated, systematic strategy — Satan is NOT disorganized. The goal: STAND (4 times in 6:11-14) — not attack but HOLD GROUND already taken.
options: So they may stand against the schemes of the devil
To fight Muslims (according to the text's framing)
To gain glory (per the textual narrative)
To learn warfare strategy

content: According to Ephesians 6:19-20, Paul asks for anointing to preach the Gospel even though he is what?
explanation: Ephesians 6:19-20 — 'and also for me, that words may be given to me in opening my mouth boldly to proclaim the mystery of the gospel, for which I am an ambassador in chains, that I may declare it boldly, as I ought to speak.' 'AMBASSADOR IN CHAINS' (presbeuō en halysei) = paradox = a ROYAL ambassador in fetters — Paul represents Christ's kingdom; Roman chains do NOT stop the mission.
options: In the midst of wealth (as described in the passage)
Being honored (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
An ambassador in CHAINS (the imperial ambassador in fetters) — still asking for boldness to preach
A king in his realm (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Ephesians 6:23-24, what is the closing benediction?
explanation: Ephesians 6:23-24 — 'Peace be to the brothers, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Grace be with all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with love incorruptible.' 'INCORRUPTIBLE' (aphtharsia) = imperishable, eternal. The closing combines: PEACE + LOVE + FAITH + GRACE — 4 core gospel words.
options: Peace + grace from Abraham (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
PEACE + LOVE WITH FAITH from the Father and Christ — GRACE be with all who love Jesus Christ with love incorruptible
Health + wealth from the king (according to the text's framing)
Wisdom + fame from the teacher (as described in the passage)
